next_step_guidance: Do not start a broad architecture search. First fix or diagnose why XA underperforms the pooled baseline under the same unseen-drug protocol (Outcome 3: residual/distillation/Phase-B last-block GIN FT).

performance_failure.implication: Architecture/training of BioCDA-XA-Candidate needs revision relative to BioCDA-Predictive baseline.

evidence.selection_data: Round19 development_rows.csv (same GDSC2 cell-line response development scope as Round20 selection)
evidence.note: Round21 REJECTED is an in-protocol unseen-drug comparison on development data, not an external GDSC/TCGA domain failure. Round20 vs Round21 also differ in split seeds (52/62/72×5fold vs 17/29/43 70/15/15), so they are not identical protocols even though both are unseen-drug.

external_domain_failure.implication: Do not interpret REJECTED as external generalization failure without a dedicated external-domain evaluation.

attention_health_failure.implication: Rejection was not driven by uniform/collapsed attention on the diagnostic batch.

context_utilization_failure.implication: Context reaches query/attention path; cannot claim context was ignored. Predictive utility of context under XA remains weak (M2≈M1).

reproduction_failure.implication: Do not treat this as pipeline/reproducibility breakage before changing models.

secondary_observations: M2 early-stopped at epoch 2 on seed 17 (AUC 0.687), suggesting unstable optimization under Phase-A freeze for that seed.
M1 also underperforms M0 (ΔAUC ≈ -0.032), so failure is not solely context integration; omics-conditioned XA itself trails pooling.
Round21 M0 is a factory reimplementation of pooled fusion, not a byte-identical reload of Round20 B_E3 ensemble checkpoints.
